workflow_simplicity_rationale: Mix of independent tasks (report writing 10%, IEP development 15%) and interdependent ones (counseling 20%, IEP meetings 8%, crisis intervention 6%). About half the work can be done standalone, but the other half requires real-time coordination with students, teams, and families. Moderate interdependence overall.
x_scale_rationale: Counseling (20%), crisis intervention (6%), and IEP meetings (8%) are in-person, one-at-a-time interactions. Report writing (10%) and IEP planning (15%) are digital and could scale. Digital task share is 0.25. The majority of the role is physically present, limiting throughput gains to modest levels.
x_sub_rationale: The therapeutic relationship IS the core product. Counseling (20% of time) depends on human trust, empathy, and rapport. Crisis intervention (6%) demands human judgment and emotional presence. Students and families receive a human relationship as the service. Interpersonal task share is 0.50. While teletherapy/AI therapy is emerging, the human psychologist is intrinsically part of what's being delivered. Strong human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Research (15%), market monitoring (10%), and report preparation (12%) are largely independent, self-contained tasks. Client meetings (20%) are scheduled and standalone. Coordination with external professionals (8%) involves handoffs but is bounded. Most tasks can be done in flexible order with some sequencing around client interactions. Mostly independent.
x_scale_rationale: Report preparation (12%), research (15%), and market monitoring (10%) are digital and meaningfully parallelizable — an AI could analyze many portfolios simultaneously. Client meetings (20%) and networking (6%) are one-on-one and not scalable. About 37% of time is clearly digital-scalable, yielding moderate throughput gains.
x_sub_rationale: Financial planning involves major life decisions (retirement, estate planning, education funding) where clients seek human trust, reassurance, and relationship. Client meetings (20%) are about emotional connection during market volatility and life transitions. Interpersonal share is 0.375. While robo-advisors handle basic portfolios, comprehensive financial planning relies on the human advisor as a trusted partner. Strong human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Tasks within each transaction follow a loose sequence (research, showings, negotiation, paperwork) but each step is largely self-contained. Market analysis (10%), research (4%), and document prep (12%) are fully independent. Property showings (20%) and negotiations (15%) are scheduled events. Cross-transaction work is completely independent. Mostly independent with light sequencing.
x_scale_rationale: Property showings (20%) are physical, one-at-a-time events. Negotiations (15%) are per-transaction. Relationship maintenance (6%) is per-person. Market analysis (14%) and document prep (12%) are digital and scalable. Digital task share is 0.25. The largest time block (showings) is physical, creating bottlenecks that limit overall throughput gains to modest levels.
x_sub_rationale: Mixed substitutability. For showings (20%) and negotiations (15%), some buyers want human advocacy and personal touch, while others prefer self-service (Zillow/Redfin model). The agent serves as a functional intermediary rather than someone whose human presence IS the product. Interpersonal share is 0.50. The service is increasingly commoditized, but the high-stakes nature of home purchases keeps many buyers wanting human guidance.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: The bulk of time is in analysis (25%) and report preparation (18%), which are independent, deep-work tasks. Research (7%) is self-contained. Client interviews (15%) are scheduled standalone events. Coordination (10%) and implementation support (5%) require more interdependence. Low heterogeneity (0.59) confirms tasks are relatively uniform in nature. Mostly independent.
x_scale_rationale: Analysis (25%), report preparation (18%), and research (7%) are all digital knowledge work that AI could parallelize across many clients or analyses simultaneously. That's 50% of time in highly scalable tasks. Client interviews (15%) are one-on-one but could be partially automated. Digital task share is 0.286. Strong throughput gains possible for the analytical core of consulting.
x_sub_rationale: Clients buy strategic insights and recommendations, not a human relationship. The deliverable is analysis and a slide deck — the output stands on its own merit. Interpersonal share is 0.286. While C-suite executives may have trust-based preferences for human consultants, the actual product (strategic analysis, market sizing, operational improvement recommendations) is evaluable independent of who produced it. Weak human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Journalists work largely independently on stories. Research/investigation (30%), writing (25%), editing (10%), and monitoring (10%) are all self-contained tasks. Interviews (15%) are scheduled standalone events. Attending events (5%) is independent. Very high task independence — the journalist assembles a story through a series of individually completable steps. Mostly independent.
x_scale_rationale: Writing (25%), editing (10%), and news monitoring (10%) are digital and parallelizable — AI could produce many articles simultaneously. Research (30%) has both digital and physical components (field reporting, document review). Interviews (15%) and events (5%) are in-person. High heterogeneity (1.07) means some tasks scale well and others don't. Moderate overall throughput gain.
x_sub_rationale: The product is the article/story, not the human journalist. Readers consume news content and generally don't care who wrote it — especially for routine news, data-driven stories, and summaries. AI-generated news articles are already commonplace. For investigative journalism with byline credibility, human authorship matters more, but this is a subset. Interpersonal share is 0.286. Weak human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Lesson planning (20%) and grading (15%) are independent, can be done in any order. But classroom instruction (35%) — the largest task — requires constant real-time adaptation, managing student questions, behavior, and engagement. Classroom management (10%) is tightly coupled with instruction. Parent communication (8%) is somewhat independent. The split is roughly 35% independent prep/grading and 45% real-time interdependent classroom work. Moderate interdependence.
x_scale_rationale: Teaching is inherently one-classroom-at-a-time. Instruction delivery (35%) is physical, in-person, with one teacher per classroom. Classroom management (10%) is physical. Parent communication (8%) is per-family. Digital task share is 0.0. Even lesson planning and grading, while partially digital, don't offer meaningful parallelization gains because the bottleneck is classroom delivery. No throughput gain.
x_sub_rationale: The human teacher IS the product in K-12 education. Children need human adults for socialization, mentorship, emotional development, and authority. Interpersonal task share is 0.571 — the highest in this batch. Parents entrust children to human teachers for developmental reasons that go far beyond content delivery. AI tutoring supplements but cannot replace the human as developmental figure, role model, and classroom leader. The human is the product.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Documentation (15%) and treatment planning (10%) are independent. Patient evaluation (20%) and training patients/families (7%) are per-session and somewhat self-contained. Collaboration with healthcare team (8%) requires coordination. Equipment maintenance (5%) is independent. Within treatment sessions, the therapist must adapt in real-time to patient responses. Mix of independent documentation and moderately interdependent clinical work.
x_scale_rationale: The core of OT is hands-on physical work with patients — evaluation (20%), direct treatment, and training (7%) are all physical, one-patient-at-a-time activities. Equipment maintenance (5%) is physical. Documentation (15%) and treatment planning (10%) are digital but secondary. Digital task share is 0.286, but the physical bottleneck of in-person therapy dominates. No meaningful throughput gain.
x_sub_rationale: Occupational therapy is hands-on: the therapist physically guides patients through exercises, assesses motor function, adapts equipment, and provides motivational support. The human touch and physical presence are intrinsic to the therapeutic process. Patients need a human body working with their body. The interpersonal relationship and physical interaction ARE the therapy. Human IS the product.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Nursing tasks are tightly coupled to real-time patient status. Direct care (35%) requires continuous monitoring and rapid response. Assessment (15%) triggers care decisions. Communication with physicians (10%) is ongoing and time-sensitive for orders and status changes. Documentation (20%) is somewhat independent but must be done promptly after interventions. Patient education (8%) is per-patient. Shift handoffs, real-time vital sign changes, and physician coordination create tight interdependence. Tightly coupled.
x_scale_rationale: Nursing is fundamentally physical bedside care. Administering medications (35%), physical assessment (15%), and equipment prep (5%) are all hands-on, one-patient-at-a-time. Digital task share is 0.143 — the lowest tier. Documentation (20%) is the only meaningfully digital task. The physical bottleneck of in-person patient care completely dominates. No throughput gain.
x_sub_rationale: The human nurse IS the care experience. Patients in hospitals are vulnerable and rely on human compassion, advocacy, and physical touch for comfort. Interpersonal task share is 0.429. Nurses administer physical care that requires human hands and provide emotional support that is intrinsic to healing. The nurse-patient relationship is a core part of healthcare delivery. Human IS the product.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Coding (35%), debugging (20%), documentation (7%), and maintenance (8%) are all self-contained tasks typically organized as tickets/issues. Performance analysis (5%) is independent. Cross-functional collaboration (10%) involves coordination but is bounded to planning sessions and reviews. Low heterogeneity (0.322) confirms tasks are uniform in nature. Engineers work in focused blocks on individual tasks with periodic coordination. Mostly independent.
x_scale_rationale: Nearly all software engineering tasks are purely digital. Coding (35%), debugging (20%), documentation (7%), maintenance (8%), and analysis (5%) are all computer-based. Digital task share is 0.429 but this dramatically understates it — virtually 100% of actual work happens on a computer. AI can trivially parallelize across multiple codebases, features, and bug fixes simultaneously. Near-unlimited throughput gain.
x_sub_rationale: The product is working software — nobody cares whether a human or AI wrote the code as long as it functions correctly, is maintainable, and meets requirements. Interpersonal share is 0.143 (only cross-team collaboration). Code quality is evaluated on its own merits: does it pass tests, is it performant, is it readable. The output is fully substitutable.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Design/CAD work (35%) is largely independent within project phases. Code research (8%), proposals (10%), and compliance review (7%) are self-contained. Site visits (15%) are scheduled standalone events. Project management (5%) requires some coordination. While there are iteration loops between design and client/regulatory feedback, each design phase is a concentrated independent block. Mostly independent.
x_scale_rationale: Design/CAD (35%), proposals (10%), code research (8%), and compliance review (7%) are digital and offer meaningful parallelization — AI could generate multiple design alternatives simultaneously or research codes for many projects at once. Site visits (15%) and project management (5%) are physical/in-person. About 60% of time is in digital-scalable tasks, but architecture requires iterative design judgment that somewhat limits pure parallelization. Moderate throughput gain.
x_sub_rationale: The product is building designs and construction documents — technical deliverables evaluated on functionality, aesthetics, and code compliance. Most commercial architecture is pragmatic: clients want compliant, buildable, cost-effective designs. Interpersonal share is 0.429 (client-facing work), but the deliverable stands on its own merit. While 'starchitects' trade on personal vision, the vast majority of architectural work is substitutable if the output quality matches. Weak human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Core of the job is intraoperative surgery requiring constant real-time adaptation and tight coordination with anesthesia, assistants, and patient response. Pre-op planning (12%) and documentation (7%) are structured, but the dominant surgical and post-op monitoring tasks (18%+ combined) are tightly coupled. GWA distribution heavy on Making Decisions and Solving Problems (2 tasks). Judgment_task_share of 0.375 confirms dynamic decision-making throughout.
x_scale_rationale: Surgery is physical, one-surgeon-one-patient-one-OR at a time. The dominant tasks -- performing surgery, administering anesthesia, post-op monitoring, patient education -- all require physical presence. Only documentation (7% time) has any digital scaling potential. The fundamental throughput constraint is the physical procedure itself.
x_sub_rationale: Patients are purchasing the surgeon's hands-on operative skill, intraoperative judgment, and clinical expertise applied to their specific anatomy. No patient would accept an AI-only oral surgery. The human surgeon IS the product -- their training, dexterity, and real-time surgical decision-making are intrinsically what's being delivered.

custom_title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
workflow_simplicity_rationale: Split workflow: structured reporting/documentation (30% of time in documentation, court reports, pre-sentence investigations) is largely independent and sequential. But the largest single task -- community monitoring and supervision (25%) -- requires dynamic, responsive interaction with offenders. Court proceedings (4%) and violation investigations (8%) add further coordination. The mix of structured reporting and dynamic supervision yields moderate interdependence.
x_scale_rationale: Community supervision (25%), violation investigations (8%), and court testimony (4%) are all in-person, one-officer-per-caseload work. Documentation and reporting (30% combined) could see some AI-assisted throughput gain. But the physical presence requirements for home visits, meetings, and court appearances create hard bottlenecks. Modest scaling in the reporting portion only.
x_sub_rationale: Probation officers exercise state authority over individuals' liberty -- a function requiring human legal accountability and discretion. The supervisory relationship with offenders, court testimony under oath, and investigative judgment all require a human actor with legal standing. While some monitoring could theoretically be automated, the authority and discretionary judgment functions are intrinsically human. Strong human preference driven by legal and institutional requirements.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: The editing pipeline is largely linear and self-contained: ingest footage, organize assets, rough cut, fine cut, audio sync, color/effects, export. The editor works independently at their workstation for most tasks. Audio sync (20% time), asset management (8%), and deliverable prep (3%) are all solo work. Director collaboration (15%) introduces iterative feedback loops, but these are periodic check-ins rather than continuous coordination. Technical pipeline is mostly independent with handoff-style collaboration.
x_scale_rationale: Film editing is entirely digital. Audio sync (20%), asset management (8%), deliverable preparation (3%), and rough cut creation (5%) are all computer-based and could be parallelized across multiple projects or versions simultaneously. AI could generate multiple edit variants for director review. The main bottleneck is creative direction and director feedback (15%), which requires synchronous human interaction. Digital_task_share of 0.375 understates the actual digital nature of all editing work.
x_sub_rationale: Film editing output is judged by the quality of the final product -- pacing, emotional impact, narrative coherence. For most commercial, corporate, and social media editing, clients evaluate the output, not the editor. Interpersonal_task_share is only 0.125. High-end feature film editors may be valued for their specific creative sensibility, but the vast majority of editing work is output-evaluated. AI-generated edits that meet quality standards would be widely accepted.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Each patient imaging session is a self-contained unit following a highly standardized protocol: greet patient, position, capture image, verify quality, document, next patient. The dominant task (performing imaging, 35%) follows repeatable procedures. Equipment maintenance (10%) is routine and scheduled. Documentation (8%) is straightforward. Collaboration with radiologists (5%) is periodic handoffs. Very structured, repetitive workflow with minimal interdependence between cases.
x_scale_rationale: The dominant task (35% time) requires physical patient positioning and equipment operation -- one tech, one patient, one machine at a time. Equipment maintenance (10%), contrast media administration (7%), and radiation safety (3%) are all physical. Even with a high digital_task_share of 0.5, the 'digital' work largely involves operating physical imaging equipment that requires patient co-presence. Documentation (8%) is the only truly scalable component.
x_sub_rationale: Patients don't care who takes their X-ray -- they want accurate diagnostic images. The 'product' is the image, not the relationship with the technologist. However, physical patient handling (positioning, comfort, contrast administration) currently requires a human body. The interpersonal component (0.25) is functional rather than relational. If robotic positioning existed, substitutability would be higher. Mixed: output is fully substitutable but physical delivery currently requires human presence.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: The dominant tasks -- legal research (25%), document drafting (20%), and case file organization (15%) -- are highly independent, self-directed work. A paralegal can prioritize and complete these in any order. Exhibit preparation (6%) is independent. The main coordination points are client/witness interviews (12%) and trial assistance (4%), which are periodic rather than continuous. Low heterogeneity (0.51 mod) confirms consistent, predictable task structure.
x_scale_rationale: Legal research (25%), document drafting (20%), case file organization (15%), and exhibit preparation (6%) are all digital, information-processing tasks that can be massively parallelized. Document review is a classic AI scaling use case -- AI can review thousands of documents simultaneously. Only client interviews (12%) and trial assistance (4%) are in-person constrained. The overwhelming majority of paralegal time is spent on digitally scalable work.
x_sub_rationale: Paralegal work is almost entirely output-evaluated: research memos, drafted documents, organized files, prepared exhibits. Clients rarely know their paralegal's name. Interpersonal_task_share is only 0.25, mostly from witness interviews. Attorneys evaluate the quality and accuracy of the work product, not who produced it. AI-generated legal research and document drafts are already being adopted in law firms. The human element is minimal.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Each insurance application is a self-contained unit of analysis: review application, analyze financials, assess risk, set premium, document decision. Very low heterogeneity (0.34 mod) confirms highly consistent task structure. The top tasks -- application analysis (25%), financial review (20%), and record maintenance (4%) -- are all independent per-application work. Communication with agents (12%) and collaboration with actuaries (8%) are periodic handoffs, not continuous coordination.
x_scale_rationale: Application analysis (25%), financial data review (20%), regulatory research (6%), and record maintenance (4%) are entirely digital and data-driven. Each application can be processed independently and in parallel. Algorithmic underwriting already processes thousands of applications simultaneously at major insurers. Only the interpersonal communication tasks (20% combined) require synchronous human interaction. The digital_task_share of 0.5 confirms strong scaling potential.
x_sub_rationale: Nobody cares whether a human or algorithm assessed their insurance risk -- they care about the accuracy of the decision, the premium level, and the speed of approval. Algorithmic underwriting is already widespread and normalized in the industry. The interpersonal_task_share of 0.25 is mostly agent/broker communication, not customer relationships. The 'product' is a risk assessment decision, which is fully substitutable by AI.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Marketing management requires regular coordination across multiple stakeholders: creative teams, vendors, sales teams, and clients. Coordination tasks account for 19% of time (team coordination 15%, sales collaboration 4%). Strategy development (25%) and market analysis (20%) can be done somewhat independently, but campaign execution requires ongoing orchestration. The interpersonal_task_share of 0.375 -- highest among the digital roles in this batch -- reflects the coordination-intensive nature of the managerial role.
x_scale_rationale: Campaign development (25%), market analysis (20%), reporting (12%), and channel evaluation (6%) are all digital tasks that can be parallelized. AI can generate multiple campaign variants, analyze data across markets, produce reports, and manage A/B tests simultaneously. The coordination bottleneck (19% combined) limits full scaling, but the majority of productive output is digitally parallelizable. Content creation in particular scales near-infinitely.
x_sub_rationale: Marketing effectiveness is measured by results: ROI, conversions, brand metrics. While strategic leadership involves some human relationship elements (interpersonal_task_share 0.375), the outputs -- campaigns, analysis, reports -- are evaluated on quality and impact, not authorship. Most stakeholders would accept AI-generated market analysis, campaign copy, and performance reports. The managerial coordination role retains some human value, but weak overall human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Each client is an independent unit of work following a predictable sequence: gather documents, analyze records, prepare return, review, file. The dominant task -- tax return preparation (35%) -- is highly structured and self-contained. Financial analysis (20%) and return review (8%) are independent. Client consultation (15%) and tax authority communication (7%) are periodic handoffs. The digital_task_share of 0.429 and low heterogeneity (0.37) confirm a consistent, largely independent workflow.
x_scale_rationale: Tax preparation (35%), financial analysis (20%), file maintenance (5%), and return review (8%) are all digital, software-based tasks that can be processed in parallel across many clients simultaneously. TurboTax and similar platforms already demonstrate massive scaling of tax preparation. Client consultation (15%) and tax authority communication (7%) require synchronous interaction but represent the minority of time. Strong digital scaling for the bulk of the work.
x_sub_rationale: Mixed picture driven by complexity segmentation. For simple tax returns, substitutability is already demonstrated -- millions use TurboTax without human involvement. For complex advisory (tax planning, audit representation, high-net-worth strategies), clients want a trusted human advisor. The interpersonal_task_share of 0.286 reflects this advisory component. The judgment_task_share of 0.143 is modest. Overall, roughly half the service is already AI-substitutable, half retains human preference.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Writing (35%) and editing (15%) are highly independent, self-directed tasks. Research (20%) requires some SME interaction but is largely self-paced. User feedback analysis (5%) and software testing (8%) are independent. Only SME collaboration (12%) requires coordination, and these are periodic consultations rather than continuous interaction. The low heterogeneity (0.41 mod) confirms a consistent task structure. Writers work independently for the vast majority of their time.
x_scale_rationale: Technical writing is purely digital work. Documentation writing (35%), editing (15%), research (20%), feedback analysis (5%), and software testing (8%) are all computer-based tasks with no physical constraints. AI can generate documentation for multiple products simultaneously, edit across documents in parallel, and research technical topics at scale. The digital_task_share of 0.429 understates the reality -- essentially all productive output is digital. Only SME consultation (12%) requires synchronous interaction. Near-unlimited parallelization.
x_sub_rationale: Technical documentation is the purest example of output-evaluated work. Users reading a manual, API doc, or installation guide care exclusively about clarity, accuracy, and completeness -- never about who wrote it. Interpersonal_task_share is only 0.143. AI-generated documentation is already common and widely accepted. The 'product' is the document, and the document is fully fungible regardless of whether a human or AI produced it.

custom_title: Child, family, and school social workers
workflow_simplicity_rationale: Case documentation (15%) and referral coordination (10%) follow structured processes and are relatively independent. But case plan development (20%) requires collaboration with clients, families, and interdisciplinary teams. Court proceedings (8%), stakeholder collaboration (6%), and ongoing monitoring (4%) add further coordination requirements. The interpersonal_task_share of 0.5 -- the highest in this batch -- reflects the coordination-intensive nature of managing cases across families, schools, courts, and agencies. Moderate interdependence overall.
x_scale_rationale: The core work is in-person: home visits, client meetings, court appearances, school consultations, and family assessments. The digital_task_share is only 0.125 -- the lowest in this batch. Documentation (15%) and referral logistics (10%) have some digital scaling potential, but the overwhelming majority of the work requires physical presence with vulnerable populations. One social worker per caseload of families, with hard in-person constraints.
x_sub_rationale: Social workers serving children and families in crisis provide trust, empathy, advocacy, and human judgment in deeply sensitive situations. The interpersonal_task_share of 0.5 is the highest in this batch. Families in crisis need a human who can read nonverbal cues, build trust over time, advocate in court with human authority, and exercise compassionate judgment about child welfare. The human relationship IS the service -- no family in a custody dispute or child protection case would accept an AI social worker.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Clinical workflow is sequential (examine -> diagnose -> treat/operate) with tasks that depend on prior findings. Surgery (20%) requires real-time coordination with vet techs. However, documentation (12%) and client education (4%) are independent. The core clinical pipeline is moderately interdependent but follows a predictable sequence, with administrative tasks that can be done separately.
x_scale_rationale: The dominant tasks are physical and in-person: examining animals (25%) and performing surgery (20%) are one-patient-at-a-time activities requiring physical presence. Even diagnostic analysis (6%) is tied to a specific physical patient. Documentation (12%) could scale but represents a small fraction. AI cannot examine or operate on multiple animals simultaneously -- physical constraints fully bottleneck throughput.
x_sub_rationale: Pet owners value their relationship with their vet and trust in the vet's judgment -- interpersonal share is 0.375. The human vet provides comfort and trust during stressful situations (sick/injured pet). However, the core product is medical care for the animal, not the human relationship per se. If robotic surgery and AI diagnostics matched quality, some owners would accept it, but many would strongly prefer a human for the empathy, communication, and hands-on care dimensions.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Real-time transcription (45% of time) is tightly coupled to courtroom proceedings -- the reporter must keep pace with spoken words in real time. However, post-processing tasks are largely independent: reviewing/editing transcripts (20%), formatting (12%), and researching terminology (4%) can be done in any order after the proceeding. Weighted across all tasks, roughly half the job is tightly coupled to live events and half is independent post-production work.
x_scale_rationale: If AI speech-to-text handles the core transcription task, it can process many audio streams simultaneously -- multiple courtrooms, depositions, or broadcasts in parallel. Post-processing tasks (review, formatting, research) are fully digital and parallelizable. The only constraint preventing near-unlimited scale is that legal transcription requires high accuracy with specialized terminology, creating some quality-checking bottleneck. But the throughput gain over a single human stenographer is strong.
x_sub_rationale: The product is an accurate legal transcript, not the human reporter. No party in a legal proceeding values the human-ness of the transcription -- they value accuracy, completeness, and legal certification. Interpersonal share is minimal (0.125). If AI-generated transcripts met accuracy and certification standards, there would be zero intrinsic preference for human production. The human is entirely substitutable in terms of what the product delivers.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Actuarial work consists of largely self-contained analytical tasks: building statistical models (25%), preparing reports (15%), monitoring compliance (8%), researching trends (4%). These follow natural project phases but each phase is fairly independent. Collaboration with underwriters (10%) and presentations (6%) are discrete coordination events, not continuous real-time interaction. Low heterogeneity (0.3764) confirms consistent, plannable task types. Most work can be organized and completed independently.
x_scale_rationale: Actuarial work is purely digital: statistical modeling, data analysis, probability calculations, report generation -- all performed on computers with no physical component. If AI can perform actuarial analysis, it can trivially run thousands of scenarios, product lines, and sensitivity analyses in parallel. Digital task share is 0.375 formally, but essentially all core tasks (analysis, modeling, reporting, compliance monitoring) are computer-based. This is near-unlimited parallelization.
x_sub_rationale: The product is accurate risk quantification and financial modeling -- outputs that are judged on correctness, not on who produced them. Interpersonal share is 0.25, limited to presentations and cross-departmental collaboration. Stakeholders care about actuarial accuracy and regulatory soundness, not whether a human performed the calculations. Some novel risk assessment requires judgment that clients might prefer from experienced humans, but the vast majority of actuarial output is fully substitutable.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Urban planning involves iterative cycles: gather community input (12%), conduct field inspections (15%), analyze data (25%+20%), coordinate with agencies (8%), then synthesize into plans and reports (10%). Tasks feed into each other -- field conditions inform analysis, public input shapes plans, departmental coordination constrains options. The work requires regular coordination across stakeholders with an interpersonal share of 0.375. Not tightly coupled in real-time, but significantly interdependent across the planning process.
x_scale_rationale: The analytical core -- reviewing proposals (25%), developing plans (20%), preparing reports (10%) -- is digital (GIS, data analysis, document preparation) and could scale. But field inspections (15%) and public meetings (12%) are physical/in-person and cannot be parallelized. Digital task share is 0.25. AI could analyze multiple development proposals simultaneously and generate plan alternatives at scale, but the community engagement and site inspection components create meaningful bottlenecks that limit overall throughput gains.
x_sub_rationale: Urban planning has a dual nature: the analytical/technical side (zoning analysis, GIS mapping, compliance review) is fully substitutable -- nobody cares if AI did the spatial analysis. But the community-facing dimension is different: facilitating public meetings (12%), stakeholder consultations, and representing planning decisions to citizens has a democratic legitimacy dimension. People expect human planners making decisions about their communities. Interpersonal share is 0.375. Mixed -- technical work is substitutable but civic engagement work benefits from human presence.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Compliance work follows regular cycles: monitoring activities (25%), preparing reports (15%), developing policies (12%), conducting training (10%), coordinating with regulators (8%). Most tasks are systematically organized and independently executable -- monitoring follows established protocols, reports have standard formats, training sessions are discrete events. Low heterogeneity (0.3563) confirms consistent task types. Some coordination is needed for regulatory interactions and cross-departmental collaboration, but most tasks can be planned and executed on their own schedules.
x_scale_rationale: Compliance monitoring and reporting are heavily document/data-based and digital. AI can simultaneously monitor compliance across many regulations, jurisdictions, departments, and business units -- significant parallelization potential. Policy development and report preparation are digital. Training (10%) and regulator coordination (8%) have in-person elements that limit scaling somewhat. Digital task share is 0.375, but the core monitoring/analysis work is almost entirely computer-based. Strong but not unlimited scaling due to interpersonal tasks.
x_sub_rationale: The product is regulatory compliance -- accurate monitoring, sound policies, and correct reporting. Organizations need compliance outcomes, not specifically a human compliance officer. If AI could monitor regulations, flag violations, generate reports, and update policies more accurately, most stakeholders would prefer it. The regulator-facing role (8%) and training delivery (10%) benefit from human interaction, but the interpersonal element is in supporting tasks, not the core product. Interpersonal share is 0.375 but mostly operational.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Instructional design follows a project-based workflow with distinct phases: needs assessment (18%), design/develop materials (25%), create e-learning (10%), evaluate programs (12%). Each phase is a largely self-contained deliverable. Collaboration with SMEs (15%) requires coordination but occurs at defined project milestones, not continuously. Training instructors (5%) is a discrete handoff event. Projects can be worked on independently of each other. The design process is plannable with clear phase boundaries.
x_scale_rationale: Instructional design is overwhelmingly digital: creating curricula, e-learning modules, multimedia content, assessments -- all computer-based content creation. If AI can design instructional materials, it can design many courses simultaneously across different subjects, formats, and learning levels. SME collaboration (15%) and instructor training (5%) require human interaction and limit perfect parallelization. But the core content creation and analysis work (73% of time) is highly parallelizable digital work.
x_sub_rationale: The end users (learners) never interact with the instructional designer -- they interact with the resulting materials. The product is effective instructional content, not the designer's human presence. Nobody taking an e-learning course knows or cares whether a human or AI designed it; they care whether it teaches effectively. Interpersonal share (0.25) is entirely about collaborating with SMEs during development, not end-user relationships. If AI-designed curricula achieved equal learning outcomes, there would be zero intrinsic preference for human-designed materials.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Logistics management is characterized by constant interdependencies: coordinating transportation (25%), monitoring inventory (20%), and supervising staff (8%) are ongoing, interacting activities. A delayed shipment ripples through inventory, staffing, and customer delivery -- decisions in one area immediately affect others. Supplier negotiations (12%) must align with operational realities. The judgment share (0.375) and interpersonal share (0.375) are both high, reflecting the reactive, multi-stakeholder coordination nature of the work. Tasks depend heavily on real-time conditions.
x_scale_rationale: The analytical dimension -- supply chain metrics (15%), inventory monitoring (20%) -- is digital and scales well; AI can optimize across many routes, warehouses, and supply chains simultaneously. But the management of physical operations, staff supervision (8%), and supplier negotiation (12%) involve real-time human interaction and physical-world constraints. Digital task share is only 0.125, the lowest in this batch. The optimization layer scales meaningfully, but the physical operations management layer creates bottlenecks.
x_sub_rationale: The product is efficient supply chain operations -- goods delivered on time, at low cost, with minimal waste. Customers and corporate leadership care about logistics outcomes, not whether a human manager orchestrated them. Many logistics optimization functions are already AI-driven (route optimization, demand forecasting, inventory management). Supplier negotiation (12%) and staff management (8%) have interpersonal elements, but the core product is operational efficiency. Interpersonal share is 0.375 but the relationship is not what's being purchased.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Retail store management is dominated by real-time, reactive activities: monitoring daily operations (25%) and managing/supervising staff (20%) are continuous, dynamic tasks where conditions change constantly -- customer traffic fluctuates, staff call in sick, inventory issues arise unexpectedly. Customer complaint resolution (12%) is inherently unpredictable. Interpersonal share is 0.50, the highest in this batch. Staffing decisions affect customer service which affects sales which feeds back to staffing -- tasks are tightly interdependent and require constant on-the-ground adaptation.
x_scale_rationale: The core management tasks are physical and in-person: floor monitoring (25%), staff supervision (20%), customer interactions (12%) all require physical presence in the store. Digital task share is 0.25, limited to data analysis (15%) and reporting (10%). While AI can optimize inventory and scheduling across many stores simultaneously (modest duplication), the in-store management work -- resolving conflicts, coaching employees, handling customer escalations -- cannot be parallelized beyond one location. Physical presence is the fundamental bottleneck.
x_sub_rationale: Mixed substitutability. The analytical/operational side (inventory optimization, sales analysis, scheduling) is fully substitutable -- AI does this better already. But the people-management dimension is substantial: coaching employees (20%), resolving customer complaints (12%), and motivating teams require human interpersonal skills. Interpersonal share is 0.50. Staff expect a human manager for performance conversations, conflict resolution, and mentorship. Customers escalating complaints often want a human. However, the ultimate product is a well-run store, and if AI could achieve that, corporate leadership would accept it.

workflow_simplicity_rationale: Manufacturing engineering is project-based with distinct phases: analyze processes (25%), design improvements, develop documentation (15%), evaluate equipment (10%), support new product introductions (7%). Each project phase is largely self-contained -- the engineer can work independently on analysis before presenting recommendations. Cross-functional collaboration (12%) occurs at defined intervals, not continuously. Interpersonal share is low (0.125). Low heterogeneity (0.5065) suggests consistent, plannable engineering tasks. Most work can be completed independently with periodic coordination checkpoints.
x_scale_rationale: The analytical and documentation work -- process analysis (25%), documentation (15%), reports (3%), equipment evaluation (10%) -- is largely digital and could scale. AI could simultaneously optimize processes across multiple production lines or facilities. But manufacturing engineering also requires physical plant observation, testing equipment on the production floor, and validating process changes in situ. Digital task share is 0.375. The design/analysis layer scales well, but physical validation and implementation create real bottlenecks that limit overall throughput to moderate gains.
x_sub_rationale: The product is optimized manufacturing processes -- higher yield, lower cost, better quality. Production teams and management evaluate manufacturing engineers on outcomes, not on the human element of the work. Interpersonal share is only 0.125, the lowest in this batch. Nobody on the production floor cares if a human or AI designed the process improvement, as long as it works. If AI could analyze production data, design tooling, and optimize workflows to achieve equal or better outcomes, acceptance would be high.
